mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of threshold-crossing instances, or t-statistic for return difference is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized monthly increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirm broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of cases, or partial R-squared for interaction term is below 0.01 in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, but this mechanism only generates positive returns during confirmed economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 20d returns in more than 55% of expansion-phase observations, or regime-split IC difference is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level exceeding 250.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate equity sell-offs that yield negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of threshold-exceeding instances, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payroll employment levels indicate robust labor market conditions that sustain corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with payroll levels.

mechanism_narrative: A widening divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals underlying demand fragility that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Divergence metric fails to predict return direction with accuracy above 52%, or interaction coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index confirm a supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk aversion, generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of consecutive-decline instances, or signal precision drops below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.
